title: Breach register maintenance and linkage

compliance.assurance_rationale: Phase-D retagged from meta-multi placeholder to a concrete AU Privacy Act clause. §26WK requires a breach register; linkage across events is primary evidence. Assurance level set to 'contributing' pending SME review per Phase E of the plan.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that AU Privacy Act §26WK (NDB — notifiable data breach) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.29.11: Breach register maintenance and linkage.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.29.11' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails, DLP system outputs, consent management platforms, data classification tools, cross-border transfer logs, privacy impact assessments, breach notification records
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
spl:
index=privacy sourcetype="breach:event" jurisdiction IN ("SG","TH","JP","KR","CN") earliest=-180d
| eval detected=strptime(detected_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval authority=strptime(notified_authority_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval individuals=strptime(notified_individuals_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval sla_h=case(jurisdiction="SG",72,jurisdiction="TH",72,jurisdiction="JP",120,1=1,24+11)
| eval breach=if(isnull(authority) AND (now()-detected)>sla_h*3600,1,0)
| eval register_line="11"
| table breach_id, jurisdiction, breach, register_line
description: Provides APAC privacy supervisory evidence for breach register maintenance and linkage aligned with APAC breach laws.
value: Provides APAC privacy supervisory evidence for breach register maintenance and linkage aligned with APAC breach laws.
implementation: (1) Maintain `apac_transfer_register.csv` and consent or DPIA lookups referenced in runbooks. (2) Map technical identifiers to records of processing activities with legal review. (3) Restrict indexes to regional privacy roles with least privilege. (4) Quarterly DPIA and consent linkage review with local counsel.
visualization: Stacked bar (jurisdiction x action), Table (open reviews), Single value (exports without mechanism).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We give you APAC privacy supervisory evidence for breach register maintenance and linkage aligned with APAC breach laws — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails, DLP system outputs, consent management platforms, data classification tools, cross-border transfer logs, privacy impact assessments, breach notification records.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain `apac_transfer_register.csv` and consent or DPIA lookups referenced in runbooks. (2) Map technical identifiers to records of processing activities with legal review. (3) Restrict indexes to regional privacy roles with least privilege. (4) Quarterly DPIA and consent linkage review with local counsel.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="breach:event" jurisdiction IN ("SG","TH","JP","KR","CN") earliest=-180d
| eval detected=strptime(detected_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval authority=strptime(notified_authority_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval individuals=strptime(notified_individuals_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval sla_h=case(jurisdiction="SG",72,jurisdiction="TH",72,jurisdiction="JP",120,1=1,24+11)
| eval breach=if(isnull(authority) AND (now()-detected)>sla_h*3600,1,0)
| eval register_line="11"
| table breach_id, jurisdiction, breach, register_line
```

Understanding this SPL

**Breach register maintenance and linkage (APAC breach laws)** — Provides APAC privacy supervisory evidence for breach register maintenance and linkage aligned with APAC breach laws.

Documented **Data sources**: Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails, DLP system outputs, consent management platforms, data classification tools, cross-border transfer logs, privacy impact assessments, breach notification records. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: privacy; **sourcetype**: breach:event. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=privacy, sourcetype="breach:event", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **detected** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **authority** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **individuals** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sla_h** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **breach** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **register_line** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Pipeline stage (see **Breach register maintenance and linkage (APAC breach laws)**): table breach_id, jurisdiction, breach, register_line


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar (jurisdiction x action), Table (open reviews), Single value (exports without mechanism).
